visualBible: Indian miniature composition, Ravi Varma-era material richness, restrained cinematic lighting; text-grounded ancient Indian clothing, weapons, ages and relationships; original fictional faces; no recognizable actors, copied frames, modern fantasy armor, anime, watermarks, embedded text, or graphic violence.

assets.prompt: Vertical full-length portrait of mature Karna, dignified sun-born warrior and king of Anga, restrained gold earrings and chest armor, amber dawn, grave generosity and isolation.
Vertical full-length portrait of mature Krishna as statesman and charioteer, yellow garments, peacock feather, composed intelligence, subtle divine luminosity without spectacle.
Vertical full-length portrait of mature Duryodhana as powerful Kuru crown prince, historically grounded mace and court dress, proud resolve rather than caricature.
Vertical full-length portrait of mature Kunti, royal mother carrying a grave secret, period sari, restrained jewelry, river-dawn palette, strength and contained sorrow.
Vertical full-length portrait of young Abhimanyu, princely archer and chariot warrior, youthful courage, historically grounded light armor, wheel motif kept symbolic.
Vertical contemplative portrait of weary adult Ashwatthama before the night raid, Brahmin-warrior garments, subtle luminous forehead gem, owl and moonlit banyan, no violence.
Wide scene of Karna's tournament recognition and Duryodhana crowning him king of Anga before the astonished Kuru court, with heroic ceremony and political consequence.
Wide non-graphic final-duel tableau: Karna beside his sunken chariot wheel, Arjuna and Krishna opposite, Shalya behind, tragic stillness before the irreversible choice.
Wide Hastinapura court scene of Krishna's failed peace embassy and restrained cosmic revelation, Duryodhana defiant and the elders confronting catastrophe.
Wide scene of Krishna as Arjuna's charioteer between the two armies before battle, conch standards and disciplined ranks, philosophical stillness rather than action.
Wide court scene of Duryodhana refusing Krishna's five-village proposal while Bhishma, Drona, Dhritarashtra and Vidura react with grave restraint.
Wide non-graphic final mace duel between Duryodhana and Bhima beside a quiet lake at dusk, Krishna and Pandavas distant, exhausted tragic resolve.
Wide intimate dawn scene of young Kunti placing infant Karna safely in a sealed woven cradle upon the river, grief and tenderness without sensationalism.
Wide sunrise meeting beside the Ganga: mature Kunti waits as Karna completes water offerings, their distance conveying revealed kinship and an impossible request.
Wide strategic view of young Abhimanyu driving toward the vast spiral chakravyuha, layered standards, elephants, chariots and disciplined ranks, determination without bravado.
Wide non-graphic final stand of exhausted Abhimanyu holding a broken chariot wheel amid distant encircling commanders, isolation and courage without visible wounds.
Wide moonlit scene of Ashwatthama, Kripa and Kritavarma beneath a banyan observing an owl among silent crows, ominous contemplation before the sleeping-camp attack.
Wide pale-dawn aftermath: Ashwatthama kneels with the surrendered gem in an open headband as Krishna pronounces judgment before survivors and Vyasa, grief without gore.
